title: Normalized Feed Domain, Content Variants, and Provenance · Stakeholder Go Mission
summary: A multilingual public-library news desk and local publishers. They need explicit source approval, safe direct and autodiscovery flows, RSS and Atom parsing, URL and namespace provenance, bounded XML, loss reports, and publisher correction evidence. Planned artifact: Deliver Authorized Community Source Registry with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, a…
objectives: Define reader group, decision, task journey, freshness need, harm, and observable value before choosing architecture.
Admit only alternate links with supported RSS or Atom media types, resolve references against the response URL, and bound candidates.
Restrict schemes, ports, redirects, resolved addresses, proxy behavior, and re-resolution to block private, local, metadata, and rebinding targets.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Project Feed Go Reader Decision
steps.instruction:
project case — A project Go team handles case 9788 during a cross-tenant privacy audit. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile format-specific source values with normalized identity, content kinds, and loss evidence; browser Go must remain deterministic and free of network, database, process, and host effects; then inject one URL, DNS, redirect, compression, entity, depth, timeout, or publisher-budget failure and reject it safely. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Project Feed Go Reader Decision.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for define reader group, decision, task journey, freshness need, harm, and observable value before choosing architecture. For Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Project Feed Go Reader Decision, keep Feed Go Reader Decision connected to executable Go feed, XML, HTTP, PostgreSQL, schedule, API, reader, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

options.text: State the Feed Go Reader Decision preconditions, preserve earlier contracts, and name the trace or changed-input result that will decide success.
Collecting many links automatically creates a useful reader product. That claim fails the commit to the mission model evidence boundary for Feed Go Reader Decision.

For commit to the mission model, import one convenient trusted RSS feed, omit reader and publisher outcomes, revision, discovery and SSRF scope, Atom and namespace cases, XML and HTTP budgets, PostgreSQL constraints and transaction, schedule, lease, goroutine cleanup, tenant, accessibility, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as feed-service proof. This does not satisfy Feed Go Reader Decision for loss-aware normalized feed and entry model.
